name: avahi-daemon
title: avahi-daemon
description:
Avahi mDNS/DNS-SD daemon
Avahi is a fully LGPL framework for Multicast DNS Service Discovery.
It allows programs to publish and discover services and hosts
running on a local network with no specific configuration. For
example you can plug into a network and instantly find printers to
print to, files to look at and people to talk to.
.
This package contains the Avahi Daemon which represents your machine
on the network and allows other applications to publish and resolve
mDNS/DNS-SD records.

arguments.description: mon will reflect its current state and the selected host name in the process title.
arguments.raw: -dae  mon will reflect its current state and the selected host name in the process title.

arguments.description: Don't chroot(2) the daemon. This option is only available when compiled with chroot()
arguments.raw: --no-chroot  Don't chroot(2) the daemon. This option is only available when compiled with chroot()

arguments.description: Don't drop root privileges after startup and don't require daemon to be started as
arguments.raw: --no-drop-root  Don't drop root privileges after startup and don't require daemon to be started as

arguments.description: Don't change the process name while running. Unless this option is specified avahi-dae-
arguments.raw: --no-proc-title  Don't change the process name while running. Unless this option is specified avahi-dae-

arguments.description: Don't enforce resource limits as specified in the configuration file. (See setrlimit(2)
arguments.raw: --no-rlimits  Don't enforce resource limits as specified in the configuration file. (See setrlimit(2)

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use avahi-daemon because Avahi mDNS/DNS-SD daemon.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use avahi-daemon as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `avahi-daemon [options]` as documented upstream for avahi-daemon.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `avahi-daemon --kill` as documented upstream for avahi-daemon.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `avahi-daemon --reload` as documented upstream for avahi-daemon.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `avahi-daemon --check` as documented upstream for avahi-daemon.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair avahi-daemon with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `avahi-daemon --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install avahi. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `avahi-daemon --kill`. Further invocations from upstream: `avahi-daemon --reload`; `avahi-daemon --check`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Avahi mDNS/DNS-SD daemon.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: avahi-daemon - The Avahi mDNS/DNS-SD daemon.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install avahi
Install with: sudo apt install avahi
